The user is asking about the difference between two apps they see — one in the simulator and one in Expo Go. Let me understand what's going on here.

From the `rork.json`, there are two apps registered:
1. `ios-ivx` — a Swift native iOS app
2. `expo` — a React Native app

The user is seeing two different apps because there are literally two separate apps in this project. Let me explain the difference clearly and concisely.

Let me check the iOS app structure and the Expo app structure to give an accurate audit of the differences.

You're seeing **two different apps** because this project has **two separate apps registered** in `rork.json`:

| | **iOS Simulator app** | **Expo Go app** |
|---|---|---|
| **Folder** | `ios-ivx/` | `expo/` |
| **Framework** | Swift / SwiftUI (native) | React Native (Expo) |
| **Bundle ID** | (Xcode project) | `com.ivxholdings.app` |
| **App name** | "Ivx" | "IVX Holdings SDK54" |
| **Runs in** | iOS Simulator (WebRTC stream) | Expo Go client |

They are **two independent codebases** that share the same backend (`backend/hono.ts`, Render deployment). They are not two views of the same app — they are genuinely different apps written in different languages, each with their own login screen, auth flow, and UI.

Let me do a quick audit of both so you know exactly what each one is and whether they're in sync.

1|import Foundation
     2|
     3|/// IVX Owner Authentication Service
     4|///
     5|/// Communicates with the IVX backend auth proxy at api.ivxholding.com.
     6|/// The proxy uses the server-side Supabase service role key to authenticate
     7|/// the owner, bypassing the missing anon key on Render production.
     8|///
     9|/// Endpoints:
    10|///   POST /api/ivx/owner-auth/login     — email + password → session
    11|///   POST /api/ivx/owner-auth/refresh   — refresh token → new session
    12|///   POST /api/ivx/owner-auth/recover   — email → reset email
    13|///   POST /api/ivx/owner-auth/repair    — email + newPassword → V7 repair
    14|///   POST /api/ivx/owner-auth/exchange-recovery — code → session (reset flow)
    15|///   POST /api/ivx/owner-auth/update-password — accessToken + newPassword
    16|///   GET  /api/ivx/owner-auth/diagnostic — backend readiness
    17|final class IVXAuthService {
    18|    static let shared = IVXAuthService()
    19|
    20|    private let baseURL: String
    21|    private let session: URLSession
    22|
    23|    private init() {
    24|        let configuredURL = (Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["IVX_API_BASE_URL"] as? String)?
    25|            .trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) ?? ""
    26|        self.baseURL = configuredURL.isEmpty ? "https://api.ivxholding.com" : configuredURL
    27|        let config = URLSessionConfiguration.default
    28|        config.timeoutIntervalForRequest = 20
    29|        config.timeoutIntervalForResource = 30
    30|        config.waitsForConnectivity = true
    31|        self.session = URLSession(configuration: config)
    32|    }
    33|
    34|    // MARK: - Login
    35|
    36|    func login(email: String, password: String) async throws -> OwnerSession {
    37|        let body: [String: Any] = [
    38|            "email": email.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces).lowercased(),
    39|            "password": password,
    40|        ]
    41|        let data = try await postJSON("/api/ivx/owner-auth/login", body: body)
    42|        let response = try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthLoginResponse.self, from: data)
    43|        guard response.ok, let session = response.session else {
    44|            throw IVXAuthError.loginFailed(response.error ?? "Authentication failed.")
    45|        }
    46|        return OwnerSession(
    47|            accessToken: session.accessToken,
    48|            refreshToken: session.refreshToken,
    49|            expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
    50|            tokenType: session.tokenType ?? "bearer",
    51|            userId: response.user?.id,
    52|            email: response.user?.email,
    53|            emailConfirmed: response.user?.emailConfirmed,
    54|            role: response.user?.role,
    55|            accountType: response.user?.accountType,
    56|            firstName: response.user?.firstName,
    57|            lastName: response.user?.lastName
    58|        )
    59|    }
    60|
    61|    // MARK: - Refresh
    62|
    63|    func refreshSession(refreshToken: String) async throws -> OwnerSession {
    64|        let body: [String: Any] = ["refreshToken": refreshToken]
    65|        let data = try await postJSON("/api/ivx/owner-auth/refresh", body: body)
    66|        let response = try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthRefreshResponse.self, from: data)
    67|        guard response.ok, let session = response.session else {
    68|            throw IVXAuthError.refreshFailed(response.error ?? "Token refresh failed.")
    69|        }
    70|        return OwnerSession(
    71|            accessToken: session.accessToken,
    72|            refreshToken: session.refreshToken,
    73|            expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
    74|            tokenType: session.tokenType ?? "bearer",
    75|            userId: nil, email: nil, emailConfirmed: nil,
    76|            role: nil, accountType: nil, firstName: nil, lastName: nil
    77|        )
    78|    }
    79|
    80|    // MARK: - Recover (send reset email)
    81|
    82|    func sendRecoveryEmail(email: String) async throws {
    83|        let body: [String: Any] = [
    84|            "email": email.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces).lowercased(),
    85|        ]
    86|        let data = try await postJSON("/api/ivx/owner-auth/recover", body: body)
    87|        let response = try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthSimpleResponse.self, from: data)
    88|        guard response.ok else {
    89|            throw IVXAuthError.recoveryFailed(response.error ?? "Could not send reset email.")
    90|        }
    91|    }
    92|
    93|    // MARK: - Repair (V7 emergency password reset)
    94|
    95|    func repairOwner(email: String, newPassword: String, phone: String? = nil) async throws -> String {
    96|        var body: [String: Any] = [
    97|            "email": email.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces).lowercased(),
    98|            "newPassword": newPassword,
    99|        ]
   100|        if let phone = phone, !phone.isEmpty {
   101|            body["phone"] = phone
   102|        }
   103|        let data = try await postJSON("/api/ivx/owner-auth/repair", body: body)
   104|        let response = try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthRepairResponse.self, from: data)
   105|        guard response.ok else {
   106|            throw IVXAuthError.repairFailed(response.error ?? "Owner repair failed.")
   107|        }
   108|        return response.message ?? "Owner password reset. You can now sign in."
   109|    }
   110|
   111|    // MARK: - Exchange Recovery Code
   112|
   113|    func exchangeRecoveryCode(_ code: String) async throws -> OwnerSession {
   114|        let body: [String: Any] = ["code": code]
   115|        let data = try await postJSON("/api/ivx/owner-auth/exchange-recovery", body: body)
   116|        let response = try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthLoginResponse.self, from: data)
   117|        guard response.ok, let session = response.session else {
   118|            throw IVXAuthError.loginFailed(response.error ?? "Recovery code exchange failed.")
   119|        }
   120|        return OwnerSession(
   121|            accessToken: session.accessToken,
   122|            refreshToken: session.refreshToken,
   123|            expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
   124|            tokenType: session.tokenType ?? "bearer",
   125|            userId: response.user?.id,
   126|            email: response.user?.email,
   127|            emailConfirmed: response.user?.emailConfirmed,
   128|            role: nil, accountType: nil, firstName: nil, lastName: nil
   129|        )
   130|    }
   131|
   132|    // MARK: - Update Password (with access token from recovery)
   133|
   134|    func updatePassword(accessToken: String, newPassword: String) async throws -> String {
   135|        let body: [String: Any] = [
   136|            "accessToken": accessToken,
   137|            "newPassword": newPassword,
   138|        ]
   139|        let data = try await postJSON("/api/ivx/owner-auth/update-password", body: body)
   140|        let response = try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthSimpleResponse.self, from: data)
   141|        guard response.ok else {
   142|            throw IVXAuthError.repairFailed(response.error ?? "Password update failed.")
   143|        }
   144|        return response.message ?? "Password updated."
   145|    }
   146|
   147|    // MARK: - Diagnostic
   148|
   149|    func fetchDiagnostic() async throws -> AuthDiagnostic {
   150|        let url = URL(string: "\(baseURL)/api/ivx/owner-auth/diagnostic")!
   151|        let (data, _) = try await session.data(from: url)
   152|        return try JSONDecoder().decode(AuthDiagnostic.self, from: data)
   153|    }
   154|
   155|    // MARK: - Private
   156|
   157|    private func postJSON(_ path: String, body: [String: Any]) async throws -> Data {
   158|        let url = URL(string: "\(baseURL)\(path)")!
   159|        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
   160|        request.httpMethod = "POST"
   161|        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
   162|        request.httpBody = try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: body, options: [])
   163|        let (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
   164|        guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
   165|            throw IVXAuthError.networkError("No HTTP response from server.")
   166|        }
   167|        // 401/403/400 still return JSON with error info — don't throw on status,
   168|        // let the caller decode the error message.
   169|        _ = httpResponse.statusCode
   170|        return data
   171|    }
   172|}
   173|
   174|// MARK: - Errors
   175|
   176|enum IVXAuthError: LocalizedError {
   177|    case loginFailed(String)
   178|    case refreshFailed(String)
   179|    case recoveryFailed(String)
   180|    case repairFailed(String)
   181|    case networkError(String)
   182|    case decodeError(String)
   183|
   184|    var errorDescription: String? {
   185|        switch self {
   186|        case .loginFailed(let msg): return msg
   187|        case .refreshFailed(let msg): return msg
   188|        case .recoveryFailed(let msg): return msg
   189|        case .repairFailed(let msg): return msg
   190|        case .networkError(let msg): return msg
   191|        case .decodeError(let msg): return msg
   192|        }
   193|    }
   194|}
   195|
   196|// MARK: - Response DTOs
   197|
   198|struct AuthSessionDTO: Codable {
   199|    let accessToken: String
   200|    let refreshToken: String?
   201|    let expiresAt: Int?
   202|    let expiresInSeconds: Int?
   203|    let tokenType: String?
   204|}
   205|
   206|struct AuthUserDTO: Codable {
   207|    let id: String?
   208|    let email: String?
   209|    let emailConfirmed: Bool?
   210|    let createdAt: String?
   211|    let role: String?
   212|    let accountType: String?
   213|    let firstName: String?
   214|    let lastName: String?
   215|}
   216|
   217|struct AuthLoginResponse: Codable {
   218|    let ok: Bool
   219|    let session: AuthSessionDTO?
   220|    let user: AuthUserDTO?
   221|    let error: String?
   222|    let maskedEmail: String?
   223|}
   224|
   225|struct AuthRefreshResponse: Codable {
   226|    let ok: Bool
   227|    let session: AuthSessionDTO?
   228|    let error: String?
   229|}
   230|
   231|struct AuthSimpleResponse: Codable {
   232|    let ok: Bool
   233|    let error: String?
   234|    let sent: Bool?
   235|    let message: String?
   236|}
   237|
   238|struct AuthRepairResponse: Codable {
   239|    let ok: Bool
   240|    let error: String?
   241|    let message: String?
   242|    let action: String?
   243|    let loginReady: Bool?
   244|}
   245|
   246|struct AuthDiagnosticBackend: Codable {
   247|    let supabaseUrlPresent: Bool?
   248|    let serviceRoleKeyPresent: Bool?
   249|    let anonKeyPresent: Bool?
   250|    let anonKeyMissing: Bool?
   251|    let authProxyActive: Bool?
   252|    let bypassesAnonKey: Bool?
   253|}
   254|
   255|struct AuthDiagnostic: Codable {
   256|    let ok: Bool?
   257|    let backend: AuthDiagnosticBackend?
   258|    let deploymentMarker: String?
   259|}

(End of file - total 259 lines)

     2|// Virtual read-only view of public environment variables available to Swift code.
     3|//
     4|// The string literals below are INTENTIONALLY EMPTY in source control —
     5|// the real values are injected at iOS build time. Do not edit this file

responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking about the difference between the two apps they see — one in the iOS simulator and one in Expo Go. I've now read all the relevant files. Let me summarize the differences clearly.

From `rork.json`, there are two registered apps:
1. `ios-ivx` — Swift/SwiftUI native iOS app
2. `expo` — React Native / Expo app

They share the same backend (`backend/hono.ts` deployed on Render).

Let me give a clear, concise audit of the differences.

Key differences I found:

**iOS App (ios-ivx) — Swift/SwiftUI:**
- 3 view files: AuthGateView.swift, LoginView.swift, MainTabView.swift
- Auth via `IVXAuthService` → talks to `https://api.ivxholding.com/api/ivx/owner-auth/*` backend proxy
- Dark gradient theme (deep indigo/black)
- Login → Recovery (password reset email) → Repair (V7 emergency password reset)
- Config.swift has empty env vars (injected at build time)
- Simple, clean SwiftUI native UI

**Expo App (expo) — React Native:**
- 226+ files in `app/` directory — massive app with tons of screens (activation-center, agent-apply, ai-gallery, buy-shares, chat-hub, contract-generator, etc.)
- Auth via `lib/auth-context.tsx` → Supabase direct + backend proxy fallback (`lib/ivx-auth-proxy.ts`)
- Login screen is 1000+ lines with extensive error handling, rate limiting, telemetry, owner recovery audit, trusted restore, server-side repair
- Bundle ID: `com.ivxholdings.app`
- Version 1.2.1, SDK 54
- Way more complex — full real estate investment platform with many features

So the answer is clear: these are two completely different apps that happen to share the same backend. The iOS simulator one is a lightweight native Swift app, and the Expo Go one is a massive React Native app with 200+ screens.

Let me give the user a clear, concise summary.

Here's the clear picture of what you're seeing:

## Two separate apps, one shared backend

| | **iOS Simulator** | **Expo Go** |
|---|---|---|
| **Folder** | `ios-ivx/` | `expo/` |
| **Framework** | Swift / SwiftUI (native) | React Native (Expo SDK 54) |
| **App name** | "Ivx" | "IVX Holdings SDK54" |
| **Bundle ID** | (Xcode-managed) | `com.ivxholdings.app` |
| **Screens** | 3 files — AuthGate, Login, MainTab | 226+ files — login, chat, AI gallery, buy-shares, contract generator, analytics, admin, etc. |
| **Login UI** | Clean SwiftUI form, dark gradient, ~300 lines | 1000+ lines — rate limiting, telemetry, trusted-restore audit, server repair, proxy fallback |
| **Auth path** | `IVXAuthService` → backend proxy at `api.ivxholding.com` | `auth-context.tsx` → Supabase direct + backend proxy fallback |
| **Recovery** | "Forgot password" + "Emergency Owner Recovery" (V7 repair) | Password reset email + proxy recover + server-side repair + trusted device restore |
| **App size** | Lightweight shell | Full real estate investment platform |

**Both apps hit the same backend** — `backend/hono.ts` deployed on Render at `api.ivxholding.com`. They share the same `/api/ivx/owner-auth/*` endpoints, the same Supabase project, and the same owner credentials.

**They are not two views of the same app.** They are two independent codebases written in different languages. The Swift app is a minimal native shell with login + tab view. The Expo app is the full-featured platform with 200+ screens for investments, chat, AI, contracts, analytics, and admin.

**Why you see two in preview:** Rork registers both in `rork.json`, so both get built and previewed. The iOS simulator streams the Swift app; Expo Go loads the React Native app.

Would you like me to:
1. **Sync the iOS app** to match the Expo app's features (add more screens)?
2. **Consolidate** to just one app and remove the other?
3. **Audit whether both login flows work** against the live backend right now?
